remaining_warnings.rationale: Inherent to a studio with no hallway buffer — the source plan also has the bath opening onto the living/sleep area. Warning, not error.

blockers_resolved.blocker: Initial overlapping-connection error at 50% on LivingArea.left for both Kitchen and Bath doors.
Critic error door_opening: Bath.right at x=6 did not share an edge with LivingArea.left at x=10 because Bath fell back to the default 6x6 (illegible source dimensions).
blockers_resolved.fix: Shifted Bath position from (0,10) to (4,10) so its right wall aligns with LivingArea.left without inventing a width/height.

notes: Extracted three rooms from blurry-source.png. Kitchen (10x10) and LivingArea / Living-Sleep (12x20) had visible-but-low-contrast dimensions (size.confidence 0.6). Bath dimensions were treated as illegible per the eval scenario — size omitted, room.confidence 0.4 — and the generator filled in the default 6x6 from DEFAULT_SIZE_BY_KIND.bathroom. Used --allow-low-confidence to honour the sub-0.5 Bath room confidence. Bath position was shifted from (0,10) to (4,10) so its right wall could share an edge with LivingArea.left; this is spatial inference from the source layout, not dimension invention. Final state: validate.mjs valid:true with 0 warnings/errors, design_critic.mjs reports 0 errors and 1 warning (bathroom_privacy) which is intrinsic to a studio. brief.json carries confidence on every numeric field. ONE verification pass after first clean validate+critic, no exploratory rewrites.
